# Welcome {.unnumbered}

This site is essentially a living lab notebook; a running collection of how I work with data over time.

It brings together my approaches, projects, and things I have learned along the way. Everything here is built openly and documented as I go. 

All data used here is simulated or publicly available, and the focus is on my **approach** and **processes** to conducting rigorous, transparent, and reproducible research, rather than on particular findings. 

I like to think of this as both a portfolio, and a working record. 

For **prospective collaborators and employers**, this serves as a transparent record of how I think through analytical problems and make decisions, from framing research questions, to choosing methods of analysis, to conducting/interpreting and communicating results. 

For **students and early career researchers**, this can potentially serve as a learning resource. Most posts include comments/annotations outlining *what* I did, but more importantly *why*. 

If you find something useful, have any questions, notice something is off/spotted an error, or just want to chat, feel free to reach out! 
You can find me on [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/amelia-henry/) or [email](mailto:amelia.henry@sydney.edu.au).
